flashcards.front: What is a Concept in machine learning?
flashcards.back: A boolean-valued function defined over a set of instances. It separates instances into positive (members of the concept) and negative (non-members).
The set of all possible hypotheses (functions) that a learning algorithm can consider to model the target concept.

flashcards.front: What is the key limitation of the Find-S algorithm?
flashcards.back: It cannot determine if it has found the only consistent hypothesis, it ignores negative examples, and it is highly susceptible to noise in the data.

flashcards.front: What does the Version Space represent?
flashcards.back: It represents the uncertainty of the learner. It is the set of all hypotheses in H that are consistent with the observed training data.
The set of assumptions that a learning algorithm uses to predict outputs for inputs it has not yet encountered. It's a necessary preference for one hypothesis over another.
